raw.objective: “to clarify the most suitable transforming factor related to the daily zonisamide dose (D) providing a steady-state serum concentration (Ct) and analysed the influences of the concomitant use of antiepileptic drugs on Ct quantitatively”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, total body water volume (VTBW), BSA, extracellular water volume (VECW), SEX, AGE, comedication with PHB, VPA, CBZ, PHT on CL Included: WT, HT, comedication with CBZ, PHT, PHB, VPA on CL
raw.observations: Phenobarbital, valproic acid, carbamazepine and phenytoine significantly lowered the Concentration/Dose ratio of zonisamide.
raw.equations: VECW=0.068*(WT**0.400)*(HT**0.633) Cipred=theta1*(theta2**PHB)*(theta3**VPA)*(theta4**CBZ)*(theta5**PHT)*(D/VECW) Cobs=Cipred D = zonisamide dose. VECW=extracellular water volume depending on an individual’s weight and height. PHB/VPA/CBZ/PHT is comedication with phenobarbital/valproate/carbamazepine/phenytoin respectively (0=no; 1=yes)

extraction_target.title: Influence of coadministered antiepileptic drugs on serum zonisamide concentrations in epileptic patients: quantitative analysis based on suitable transforming factor.
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to clarify the most suitable transforming factor related to the daily zonisamide dose (D) providing a steady-state serum concentration (Ct) and analysed the influences of the concomitant use of antiepileptic drugs on Ct quantitatively”

covariates.raw: Tested: WT, total body water volume (VTBW), BSA, extracellular water volume (VECW), SEX, AGE, comedication with PHB, VPA, CBZ, PHT on CL Included: WT, HT, comedication with CBZ, PHT, PHB, VPA on CL

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
